summary: Typosquat of @solana/web3.js with install-time host reconnaissance and exfiltration
details: Package name impersonates the legitimate @solana/web3.js by using the @solana-labs scope and a hyphen-variant name. On install, the postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node install.js" in package.json) executes install.js, which collects host identifiers (hostname references on lines 10/25/27), issues an outbound HTTP POST (line 46), and invokes curl (line 103) — a network-and-shell-command pattern consistent with installer host fingerprinting and data exfiltration to an attacker-controlled endpoint. The package's only on-install effect is this reconnaissance/exfiltration script; there is no legitimate Solana SDK functionality justifying these operations at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
